The model rates Turkey as the slight favorite, assigning a 36% win probability compared to the USA's 32%. The underdog scenario is narrow; a Turkish victory is marginally more likely than an American one, but neither side commands confidence.

The draw carries substantial weight at 31%, suggesting the model expects a closely matched contest. The three-way spread is tightly clustered: no outcome falls below 31% or exceeds 37%. This points to genuine competitive balance rather than a decisive mismatch, with all three results plausible under the model's framework.

No sportsbook consensus or derivatives market has yet formed around this match, so direct comparison between the model and market odds is not possible at this stage.
